paper_metadata.title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
paper_metadata.abstract: Scaling language models with more data, compute and parameters has driven significant progress in natural language processing. For example, thanks to scaling, GPT-3 was able to achieve strong results on in-context learning tasks. However, training these large dense models requires significant amounts of computing resources. In this paper, we propose and develop a family of language models named GLaM (Generalist Language Model), which uses a sparsely activated mixture-of-experts architecture to scale the model capacity while also incurring substantially less training cost compared to dense variants. The largest GLaM has 1.2 trillion parameters, which is approximately 7x larger than GPT-3. It consumes only 1/3 of the energy used to train GPT-3 and requires half of the computation flops for inference, while still achieving better overall zero-shot and one-shot performance across 29 NLP tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM's primary innovation is the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, which allows for efficient scaling of language models to large sizes while maintaining low inference cost by activating only a subset of experts per token.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models to large sizes without increasing inference cost
extraction_data.application: Natural language generation (NLG) and natural language understanding (NLU) tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM's primary innovation is the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, which allows for efficient scaling of language models to large sizes while maintaining low inference cost by activating only a subset of experts per token.

extraction_data.innovation: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture for efficient scaling of language models, where only a small fraction of the total parameters are activated for each prediction, reducing computational cost while maintaining performance.
extraction_data.application: Natural language generation (NLG) and natural language understanding (NLU) tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models with reduced computational cost

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM introduces a mixture-of-experts architecture that allows for efficient scaling of language models by activating only a subset of experts per token, reducing computational cost while maintaining performance.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Mixture of web text, books, and other sources
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on language understanding and generation tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models to larger sizes without a proportional increase in parameters
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, and other language tasks
